item.title: Iran war "re-escalation risks" hike oil prices after latest Trump threat
item.description: Oil prices jump again on Iran war "re-escalation risks," despite Tehran saying it has shared another set of revised peace deal terms with the U.S.

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda declared global health emergency
item.description: At least 80 deaths have been reported in a new Ebola disease outbreak in Congo and Uganda, authorities said.

item.title: Divers find bodies of 4 Italians in Maldives sea cave
item.description: Italy's Foreign Ministry said Monday rescuers have located the bodies of four Italian divers who were believed to be deep inside an underwater cave in a Maldive atoll.

item.title: Fighter jets crews eject from air show crash: "A lot of luck involved"
item.description: The Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho was locked down following the midair crash during the Gunfighter Skies Air Show.

item.title: LIRR riders brace for long commutes as strike enters its third day
item.description: A late-night meeting between the MTA and union leaders failed to end a strike that shut down the Long Island Rail Road. Negotiations will continue Monday morning.

item.title: Child among 10 people shot dead by gunmen in Mexico
item.description: Nine people died at the scene from gunshot wounds and one woman died while being transported to the hospital, prosecutors said.

item.title: Read full episode transcripts of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" from 2026
item.description: Browse all of our full episode transcripts from 2026 of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan."

item.title: 2 Americans arrested over stunt at viral baby monkey Punch's zoo in Japan
item.description: Two Americans were arrested in Japan after social media posts showed a person jumping into an enclosure at the zoo where viral baby monkey Punch found fame.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrives in Netherlands
item.description: The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak​​ has docked at the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection, wrapping up a troubled journey that put world health authorities on alert.

item.title: Trump says "Clock is Ticking" for Iran as shaky ceasefire continues
item.description: President Trump said "there won't be anything left of them" if Iranian leaders do not "get moving, FAST."

item.title: Great white shark kills spearfishing diver: "Horrific event"
item.description: The 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with friends when he was attacked on a coral reef off Rottnest Island, police said.

item.title: Inside the "troubled teen" industry, when help sometimes does more harm
item.description: A CBS News investigation into youth residential treatment programs finds allegations of abuse, a lack of federal oversight, and families left to navigate a multibillion-dollar industry largely on their own.

item.title: A youth treatment center promised to help struggling teens. Some say they left with more trauma.
item.description: Youth residential treatment programs promise to help struggling teens develop coping skills and return home to become productive members of their communities. A CBS News investigation looks at whether these programs, which are largely unregulated by the federal government, are helping teens.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 10 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Religious anti-abortion center finds opportunity in town without OB-GYNs
item.description: A crisis pregnancy center in Sandpoint, Idaho, wants to expand women's healthcare three years after the labor and delivery unit at the town's hospital closed and its OB-GYNs moved out of state.

item.title: LIRR unions, MTA's late-night bargaining session ends with no deal to stop strike
item.description: With the LIRR strike entering its third day, the MTA and union leaders will resume talks at 7:30 a.m. Monday.

item.title: Austin shooting spree: 3 held after at least 12 incidents, police say
item.description: Mayor Kirk Watson said no motive has been identified and the shootings appear to be random.

item.title: Birding may hold key to keeping your brain sharp in old age, new study suggests
item.description: New research suggests birding may be good for the brain. There's evidence it could help guard the brain against dementia and age related cognitive decline. Bradley Blackburn reports.

item.title: 5/17/2026: Betting on War; The Knowledge; Christopher Nolan
item.description: First, prediction market bets on war defy odds. Then, London's cabbies skeptical about robotaxis. And, Christopher Nolan: The 60 Minutes Interview.

item.title: At least 6 Americans in Congo were exposed to Ebola virus, sources say
item.description: The World Health Organization this week declared the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a "public health emergency of international concern."

item.title: Ebola outbreak declared a global health emergency by WHO
item.description: Outbreaks of the deadly Ebola virus in two African countries have prompted the World Health Organization to declare a global health emergency. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: Trump's approval ratings on the economy continue to fall in latest CBS News poll
item.description: President Trump's approval rating is now at the lowest level of his second term and only 27% of Americans approve of how he is handling inflation, according to a new CBS News poll. Olivia Rinaldi reports.

item.title: Fighter jets collide in midair at Idaho air show
item.description: Two fighter jets collided in midair Sunday during an air show in Idaho. Officials say the crewmembers are in stable condition after they ejected.

item.title: LIRR strike has many worrying about Monday's commute
item.description: While the LIRR work stoppage has caused a mess for people heading to weekend events​, the real concern is Monday morning's commute.

item.title: Anderson Cooper's farewell to 60 Minutes
item.description: In a heartfelt sign-off, Cooper reflects on the stories, risks, adventures, and human connections that defined his two decades with television's most iconic newsmagazine.

item.title: Nate Bargatze says "American comedy shines light into dark places in a digestible way"
item.description: Nate Bargatze says a laugh can be found almost anywhere in the U.S., on nearly any topic out there. "When done right, American comedy shines light into dark places in a digestible way," he said.

item.title: Christopher Nolan: The 60 Minutes Interview
item.description: Christopher Nolan, director of "Oppenheimer," "Inception," "Interstellar," and "The Dark Knight," imagines every movie is the last he'll make, leading him toward an ambitious plan for "The Odyssey."

item.title: London cabbies not ready to hand over their keys to AI-powered, autonomous taxis
item.description: London black cab drivers, who are required to memorize thousands of streets to get their license, are being tested in a new way. Several companies are trying to bring robotaxis to the city's streets.

item.title: High win rate of bets on military operations a likely sign of insider trading
item.description: Prediction market users are profiting from bets on war. Analysts say suspiciously timed bets, and the high win rates of wagers on military outcomes , are likely signs of insider trading.

item.title: Full transcript of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 17, 2026
item.description: On this "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" broadcast, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and Ambassador Alexander Yui, Taiwan's Representative to the U.S., join Margaret Brennan.
